_meta.direction: Civic-record. A serious workplace-safety instrument that reads like a labour-tribunal binder rendered for a phone: high-contrast, dense type, restrained color, no decorative motion. Builds trust by feeling consequential and audited, not friendly. Closer to a court filing system than to a SaaS dashboard.
references: GOV.UK Design System — neutral grays, restrained palette, content-forward typography, accessibility-first density; we borrow the typographic posture, the focus-ring discipline (visible black-on-yellow outline), and the unapologetic plainness of language.

Stripe Dashboard — calm semantic color (a single accent + clearly-coded states), confident empty/loading/error treatments, and the convention that destructive surfaces explicitly name the irreversible thing about to happen. We borrow the destructive-confirm pattern.

Signal Desktop — sensitivity badges, lock screen, the conspicuous 'sealed' vocabulary that tells the user what the system can and cannot see. We borrow the visual treatment of C3/C4 sensitivity and the always-visible lock/identity affordance.
anti_patterns: Gradients, glass-morphism, or marketing-grade hero illustrations on any surface that handles concerns, reprisal, work-refusal, s.51 evidence, exports, or sessions. The product is not aspirational.
Icon-only controls without text labels for any destructive, sensitive, or export action. Icons may accompany text; they may not replace it.
Color-only state signals. Sensitivity, success, error, warning, queued, and offline always pair color with an icon AND a text label (color-blind safe; matches the AODA hard rule).
_meta.audience: Worker JHSC reps and the worker co-chair, in Ontario workplaces of 50+. Devices: personal Android phones (often older, weak signal on shop floor) and personal laptops (occasional). Context: read on the floor between tasks (gloves, one-handed, distracted, time-pressured); written from home or break room. Users include workers with disabilities — AODA is a hard constraint.
_meta.primary_task: Capture an inspection, concern, or reprisal entry quickly and confidently from a phone — and, for the co-chair, complete a deliberate, friction-by-design export to the employer co-chair without ever leaking concern-derived or C4 content.
_meta.content_shape: Forms-heavy (intake, inspection checklists, recommendations), list-heavy (concerns register, audit feed, sessions), and a small number of high-consequence interstitials (export, panic-wipe, 4-eyes). Light on data viz; no marketing copy.
_meta.tone: Plain, declarative, never coy. Names the risk. Tells the user what the system can see, what it cannot, and what it logs. Avoids euphemism on sensitive surfaces ('reprisal', 'export to employer co-chair', 'wipe this device') in favour of the literal verb.
_meta.accessibility: WCAG 2.0 AA minimum (AODA); target 2.1 AA where feasible (plan §9). Body text ratios target ≥7:1 (AAA) in both modes; small text never below 4.5:1.
_meta.dark_mode: Required. Both modes designed simultaneously; no later retrofit.
_meta.density_default: comfortable for mobile (touch target 44px floor); compact available for desktop data-table views (audit feed, sessions, concerns list).

color._principle: A single accent (slate-indigo), three semantic colors (success/warning/danger), and two sensitivity tokens (C3 / C4). C4 is visually distinct from any state color so a sensitive surface is never mistaken for a warning. Every color used is paired with a text label or icon — color carries no information alone.

sensitivity._comment: C3 = committee-encrypted (concerns, minutes drafts, inspections, recommendations). C4 = highest sensitivity (reprisal log, work refusal, s.51, source identity). Distinct hues from state.* so a sensitivity badge is never confused with a warning.

focus_ring._comment: Two-layer ring: outer high-contrast halo + inner foreground line. Visible against any background per AODA 2.4.7. Never replaced by outline:none.

onboarding._comment: T19 / ADR-0020 — wizard chrome, step-indicator states, recovery-passphrase reveal pair, browser-baseline badges, panic-wipe overlay. All pairs verified in _contrast_audit. Step-indicator states: pending (not yet reached), active (current), complete (already traversed). 'complete' deliberately re-uses state.success (positive affordance, color-blind safe via accompanying checkmark icon — see design-system §4.D.T19).

T19 / ADR-0020 — dark-mode equivalents. Same semantic naming as light.onboarding. The panic overlay in dark mode uses near-black with high opacity to drop residual UI behind a clear visual lock during the wipe.

_contrast_audit._comment: Every fg/bg pair used in component specs. Ratios computed against WCAG 2.x relative-luminance. Body text floor 4.5:1, large/UI floor 3:1. Aim AAA (7:1) for body where direction permits. TWO-LAYER FOCUS RING IS LOAD-BEARING: removing the inner ring layer is forbidden. The two-layer construction (yellow halo + dark inner line) is the WCAG 1.4.11 conformance path; the outer halo alone does not satisfy 3:1 against C3/C4 or state backgrounds. The inner-line ratio is what carries visibility on sensitive surfaces.

light.foreground.muted on background.primary: 4.8:1 AA — token darkened to #6c7280 per a11y-review B-1; suitable for body and hint text
light.foreground.disabled on background.primar: 2.7:1 — disabled-text exception; pair with disabled icon and aria-disabled
light.focus_ring.outer on background.primary: 1.9:1 — paired with inner #16181d for combined 16.1:1; ring is visible by virtue of the two-layer construction
light.focus_ring.outer on sensitivity.c4_bg: 1.3:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~15.4:1 against C4 bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.outer on sensitivity.c3_bg: 1.4:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~14.9:1 against C3 bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.outer on state.danger_bg: 1.5:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~13.7:1 against danger bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.outer on state.warning_bg: 1.2:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~13.9:1 against warning bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.outer on state.success_bg: 1.6:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~14.2:1 against success bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.outer on state.info_bg: 1.5:1 — outer alone insufficient; visibility carried by inner #16181d at ~14.6:1 against info bg (two-layer mandatory)
light.focus_ring.inner on sensitivity.c4_bg: 15.4:1 AAA — load-bearing layer for WCAG 1.4.11 on C4 surfaces
light.focus_ring.inner on sensitivity.c3_bg: 14.9:1 AAA — load-bearing layer for WCAG 1.4.11 on C3 surfaces
light.onboarding.step_indicator_pending_fg on: 6.0:1 AAA (large) / AA (small) — pending step labels are typography.roles.caption (UI text, ≥3:1 floor satisfied; 4.5:1 floor for text satisfied)
light.onboarding.step_indicator_active_fg on s: 8.6:1 AAA — current-step pill (accent.default ground); matches button primary pair
light.onboarding.step_indicator_complete_fg on: 5.7:1 AA — completed-step pill (state.success ground); never color-only — always paired with checkmark icon per design-system §4.D.T19
light.onboarding.passphrase_reveal_fg on passp: 16.1:1 AAA — load-bearing for the recovery-passphrase ceremony (D.4 reveal); ADR-0003 Amendment F §Operational rule 5 'largest typography token + highest-contrast pairing'
light.onboarding.browser_baseline_pass_fg on b: 5.7:1 AA — pass-state badge; paired with check icon + 'Supported' text
light.onboarding.browser_baseline_fail_fg on b: 6.6:1 AA — fail-state badge; paired with X icon + 'Too old' text
light.onboarding.device_fingerprint_fg on devi: 9.6:1 AAA — D.1 UA+platform render is human-inspection text in typography.roles.code
light.onboarding.completion_success_fg on comp: 5.7:1 AA — D.7 'You're set up' affirmative banner; paired with checkmark icon
light.onboarding.panic_overlay_fg on panic_ove: 15.3:1 AAA — panic-wipe in-progress overlay; on near-opaque black; loading text MUST remain legible while every other UI element drops behind
light.onboarding.download_affordance_fg on bac: 9.2:1 AAA — D.4 'Download encrypted recovery blob' secondary-affordance label (accent.default ground)
light.focus_ring.inner on onboarding.passphras: 16.1:1 AAA — focus ring inner line on the reveal surface (load-bearing for keyboard-only ceremony)
light.focus_ring.inner on onboarding.panic_ove: 1.0:1 — inner #16181d is invisible on near-black overlay; on this surface the outer #fbbf24 carries visibility at 13.1:1 (inversion of the standard two-layer rule). Implementer MUST render the inner ring layer at color.foreground.primary (i.e., #fbbf24 INVERTED to white on dark overlay) — see design-system §4.D.T19 panic-overlay-focus-ring rule

dark.border.focus on background.primary: 11.7:1 AAA (focus ring outer in dark mode is fbbf24 → high)
dark.focus_ring.outer on sensitivity.c4_bg: 9.4:1 AAA — outer alone meets 3:1 in dark mode; inner #f3f4f6 at ~10.8:1 against C4 bg provides redundancy
dark.focus_ring.outer on sensitivity.c3_bg: 9.1:1 AAA — outer alone meets 3:1 in dark mode; inner #f3f4f6 at ~10.5:1 against C3 bg provides redundancy
dark.focus_ring.outer on state.danger_bg: 8.7:1 AAA — outer alone sufficient; inner layer mandatory for two-layer construction consistency
dark.focus_ring.outer on state.warning_bg: 8.9:1 AAA — outer alone sufficient; inner layer mandatory for consistency
dark.focus_ring.outer on state.success_bg: 9.2:1 AAA — outer alone sufficient; inner layer mandatory for consistency
dark.focus_ring.outer on state.info_bg: 9.0:1 AAA — outer alone sufficient; inner layer mandatory for consistency
dark.focus_ring.inner on sensitivity.c4_bg: 10.8:1 AAA — load-bearing layer for WCAG 1.4.11 on C4 surfaces in dark mode
dark.focus_ring.inner on sensitivity.c3_bg: 10.5:1 AAA — load-bearing layer for WCAG 1.4.11 on C3 surfaces in dark mode
dark.onboarding.step_indicator_complete_fg on: 9.6:1 AAA — completed-step pill (dark state.success ground); paired with checkmark icon
dark.onboarding.passphrase_reveal_fg on passp: 15.2:1 AAA — load-bearing for the recovery-passphrase ceremony in dark mode
dark.onboarding.panic_overlay_fg on panic_ove: 15.2:1 AAA — dark-mode panic overlay near-black ground; loading text legibility load-bearing

_contrast_audit.verified_on: 2026-05-22 — designer self-audit + accessibility-specialist re-verification on B-1/B-2 fixes.

typography._principle: System fonts only. No Google Fonts, no Adobe Fonts, no @font-face at runtime (constraints.md hard rule). Display and body share the same stack; we differentiate hierarchy with size, weight, and tracking — not family. Monospace for code, key fingerprints, audit-log hashes, and one-time codes (TOTP).

family.sans: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
family.mono: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', 'Cascadia Mono', Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace
family.display: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif

typography.scale_ratio: 1.2 (minor third) — restrained; this is not a marketing site

roles._comment: Named text roles. Implementers compose from these, not from raw size/weight pairs.

_max_width_notes.recovery_sheet_print: T19 / design-system §4.D D.4.print — half a US-letter sheet (5.5in × 8.5in landscape OR 5.5in column on letter). Sized so a single sheet of paper holds the passphrase + salt + warning at typography.roles.totp scale without forcing line-wrap on the word-chunks. Print stylesheet ONLY; on-screen fallback uses layout.max_width.form.
_max_width_notes.wizard_chrome: T19 — wizard chrome max width. Wider than form (560px) because the step indicator + body + sticky action footer share the same gutter. Falls back to 100% below breakpoint.md per layout pattern 5.4 (interstitial layout).

_notes.c4_stripe: C4 sensitivity left-border + reprisal/concern card stripe; resolves G-T08-17 carry-forward (was raw '4px' in ConcernIntakeForm.svelte:446).
_notes.step_indicator: T19 wizard step-indicator pill border; active/complete/pending all use the same width.

shadow._dark_note: In dark mode, shadows are visually weaker; surfaces lift via border.subtle + background.secondary contrast rather than shadow. Modal shadow remains for layering.

motion._principle: Restrained. No bounce on any sensitive surface. No animated decoration. Motion conveys causality (this caused that) — not flair.

_duration_aliases._comment: Semantic aliases over duration.*. step_transition === slow today; lives as a named binding so a future designer pass can retune wizard pacing without re-sweeping every consumer. Implementer MUST reference the alias, not the raw duration, on T19 step-to-step transitions.

motion._no_spring: Spring/bounce easings are deliberately excluded. They are inappropriate for a labour-safety tool.
motion._reduced_motion: When prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all transitions collapse to duration.instant; opacity-only transitions persist at duration.micro for state-change feedback; no transform-based motion at all (no slide-in, no fade-up). Skeletons stop pulsing and render as flat solid blocks. Toasts appear and dismiss without translate; opacity 0→1.

spinner_rotate.reduced: replaced by static 'Loading…' text + indeterminate progress bar (no rotation)

wizard_step_transition.reduced: instant — no opacity fade; the next step renders flat, current step is removed; SR announcement still fires per design-system §4.D.T19

z_index._onboarding_note: onboarding_overlay (1550) sits ABOVE tooltip (1500) and BELOW lock_screen (1600). Semantics: T19 wizard chrome is a full-viewport surface (not a modal — there is no underlying app shell yet for a first-launch user) but is NOT a lock — a lock implies prior authenticated state. A user mid-onboarding who triggers a 15-min idle timeout MUST see the lock-screen overlay drawn on TOP of the wizard, and panic-wipe MUST draw on top of everything. Z-order intentionally preserves this lock/panic precedence.

_intent.2xl: wide desktop (rare for this app; supported)

touch_target._comment: WCAG 2.5.5 minimum is 44×44 px. Inspection workflow assumes gloves, one-handed use, distraction — bumped to 48 px minimum on every interactive control inside the inspection and reprisal pipelines. Audit feed and settings allow 44 px.

icon._comment: Inline SVG only (no third-party icon font, no CDN — constraints.md). 1.5px stroke at 24px; scales by viewBox. Always paired with text on destructive, sensitive, or export controls.

components._comment: Detailed component specs (states, triggers, a11y notes, empty/loading/error coverage) live in /home/user/agent-os/.context/design-system.md. The JSON below captures token-level decisions only — never raw values; only refs to tokens above.

behavior.initial_focus: first focusable element OR the destructive button's CANCEL when destructive_confirm
behavior.escape_dismiss: Always dismissible by Escape EXCEPT export_interstitial during re-auth and four_eyes_pending (dismiss requires explicit Cancel)
behavior.click_outside_dismiss: Allowed for dialog; FORBIDDEN for destructive_confirm, export_interstitial, reauth_prompt, passphrase_prompt, four_eyes_pending — these require explicit Cancel

auto_dismiss_ms.sensitive_activity: never (manual dismiss; also persists to audit feed)

behavior.stacking: newest at top; older fade in opacity

alert_banner._comment: In-page persistent advisory (e.g., 'You are offline — entries queued'). Not a toast; lives inside the page chrome.

form.required_indicator: trailing red asterisk + visually hidden 'required' text for SR
form.error_text_position: below input, replacing helper when present
form.validation_timing: on-blur for inline fields; on-submit for batch; passphrase / totp always validates on-submit only (never reveals correctness mid-typing)

status_pill._comment: Small inline status indicator (offline, queued, draft, in-progress, resolved, sensitive-c3, sensitive-c4). Always icon + text; never color-only.

empty_state._comment: Required for every list surface. Three elements: short heading, one-line plain-language explanation, primary action (or, where no action is possible, a single hint).

skeleton.reduced_motion: flat solid block (no shimmer), text 'Loading…' below

error_state._comment: Required for every failed network/crypto/permission path. Names the error in plain language, says what the user can do (Retry / Re-auth / Re-enter passphrase / Wait), and links to support contact for unrecoverable errors.

navigation._comment: Mobile: bottom tab bar (3-4 items max) + top header. Desktop: top header only (side nav reserved for v2 if needed). Each nav target has icon + text label; never icon-only.

components_extended._comment: JHSC-specific surfaces — not general components. State spec for each lives in design-system.md.

panic_wipe._f53_contract: F-53 destructive_confirm — literal-phrase input is keystroke-gated until ready promise resolves; pre-ready synthesized Enter does NOT fire wipe; Escape does NOT dismiss. See design-system §3.2 + §4.D.T19 PanicWipeModal.

onboarding_wizard._comment: T19 / ADR-0020 — OnboardingFlow wizard chrome + step indicator + per-step nav. State spec lives in design-system.md §4.D.T19. The wizard renders a full-viewport chrome on first-launch (no app shell yet) at z_index.onboarding_overlay. Lock-screen and panic-overlay draw above the wizard if triggered mid-flow.
onboarding_wizard._d5_canonical: Per ADR-0020 Decision 2.a + designer's pass 2026-05-24: D.5 canonical = SESSION-REVOCATION PRIMER. The print sheet is D.4.print (a sub-step rendered via @media print stylesheet). Resolves G-T19-2 labelling drift.

step_indicator.icon_complete: checkmark (icon.size.sm) — REQUIRED in addition to the success color (color-blind safe)

step_transition.reduced_motion: instant — no opacity fade; SR announces step change via role=status

test_only_props._comment: Test-only — runtime-stripped in production per ADR-0020 Decision 8. Source references via split-form: '__test_' + 'step'.
test_only_props.__test_user_agent: UA override for browser-baseline gate; ignored when MODE === 'production'

browser_baseline_badge._comment: T19 / ADR-0020 — D.3 browser-baseline pass/fail badge. Always icon + text; never color-only.

device_fingerprint_card._comment: T19 / D.1 — UA + platform string render. NEVER includes IP, geolocation, navigator.connection, Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version-List. Human-inspection only; never crosses a trust boundary.

recovery_passphrase_reveal._comment: T19 / D.4 — on-screen passphrase reveal (hold-to-reveal per Amendment F). Composes RecoveryPassphraseScreen.svelte. Largest typography token + highest-contrast pair per ADR-0003 Amendment F operational rule 5.
recovery_passphrase_reveal._forbidden: No copy-to-clipboard control; no SpeechSynthesis; no screenshot-friendly affordance; no <input> rendering of the plaintext. Static-lint enforced per ADR-0003 Amendment F operational rule 4.

recovery_blob_download._comment: T19 / D.4 — secondary off-device-copy affordance. The downloaded JSON is the ENCRYPTED blob + kdf_params metadata; NEVER the passphrase. ADR-0020 Decision 3.a Option C.

session_revocation_primer._comment: T19 / D.5 (canonical) — primer for revoking other sessions during enrollment. NOT the full Surface H list; an informational primer with a single 'Revoke other sessions' destructive secondary action and a 'Skip — I'll do this later' tertiary. Composes lib/auth/session.ts listSessions + revokeAllSessions.
session_revocation_primer._f39_contract: revokeAllSessions resolves only after server confirms ≤5s propagation (post-T05.1). UI success state lands when promise resolves; toast confirms count revoked.
session_revocation_primer._empty_state: If only the current session exists, list shows the single 'This device' row and the primer copy explains there's nothing else to revoke; Skip becomes the only forward action (still navigates to D.7).

completion_summary._comment: T19 / D.7 — terminal 'You're set up' summary. Lists what was accomplished (passkey set, recovery sheet printed-and-verified, browser supported, device flagged personal) AND points to where panic-wipe + sessions live for future need (per ADR-0020 Decision 3.f).
completion_summary.icon: check-circle (icon.size.lg) — REQUIRED in addition to color
